task: I need to send a quick note to my supervisor about a schedule change before the shift starts. Can you help me put together a short message that covers the basics and is easy to send?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a plain, short version you can text:

**“Hi [Name], I wanted to let you know about a schedule change before the shift starts. I’m still available for [part of shift], but I can’t make [other part]. Let me know what works best.”**

If you want it even shorter:

**“Hi [Name], I have a schedule change and can only make part of my shift today. Let me know what you want me to cover.”**

If you want, I can make it:
- more casual
- more professional
- firmer about the exact hours

user_simulator.text: Yeah, close. But make it a little more direct, less wordy. I need something I can send fast before I head in. Also put in that I’m sorry for the short notice, but keep it plain. Something like that.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a more direct version:

**“Hi [Name], sorry for the short notice, but I have a schedule change and can only make part of my shift today. Let me know what you want me to cover.”**

Shorter version:

**“Hi [Name], sorry for the short notice — I have a schedule change and can only work part of my shift today. Let me know what you need.”**

If you want, I can make it:
- even shorter
- more casual
- more firm about the hours

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short and plain enough.
